Monday, May 18, 2026 Daily Digest

OpenAI's $4B Enterprise Push, 51% of B2B Buyers Start with ChatGPT, Legacy SaaS Crashing

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

OpenAI's $4B Enterprise Push Signals the End of "Experimental AI"

OpenAI launched the "Deployment Company" — a $4B+ enterprise operation embedding AI engineers directly into businesses. This shifts AI from "pilot project" to operational infrastructure. Enterprise revenue now represents 40%+ of OpenAI's total and is expected to reach parity with consumer revenue by year-end. The bigger story: the AI race is no longer about the smartest chatbot — it's about who becomes embedded deepest in enterprise operations.

Read on Times Square Chronicles →
2

G2: 51% of B2B Buyers Now Start Research with AI Chatbots

G2's "Answer Economy" study is a watershed moment: 51% of B2B software buyers now begin their purchasing journey in an AI chatbot (up from 29% a year ago). ChatGPT dominates at 63% share. Even more striking: 69% said an AI chatbot led them to select a different vendor than planned, and 1/3 bought from a vendor they'd never heard of. AI-referred traffic converts at 4-5x traditional organic. The funnel just inverted.

Read on TechEdge AI →
3

SaaStr: The Real Reason B2B Stocks Are Crashing — "The Software Just Isn't Good Enough"

Jason Lemkin's brutal take: legacy B2B software is "actively worse than the alternatives now, and customers have finally noticed." The case study? Marketo's unsubscribe link broke for 2+ weeks (a CAN-SPAM violation), and Adobe's support blamed everything but the product. SaaStr's team built a fix themselves in an afternoon using Replit + Claude. The punchline: "When AI agents need state-of-the-art APIs and your 2018 API can't deliver, that's reason enough to leave."

Read on SaaStr →
4

79% of B2B Buyers Use AI Search — Traditional SEO Is Breaking

Improvado's 2026 trends report confirms the shift: 79% of B2B buyers now use AI tools like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Bing Copilot for research. Zero-click searches hit 57% of all queries. Google AI Overviews appear in 13% of results. The optimization strategy shifts from "rank on Google" to "be cited by AI." The new signals: entity salience, structured data, transparent sourcing, and unlinked brand mentions (correlation with AI citation: 0.664 vs. 0.2 for backlinks).

Read on Improvado →
5

Anthropic Overtakes OpenAI in Enterprise Adoption

Per Ramp's AI Index, Anthropic recently passed OpenAI in business adoption for the first time — driven heavily by Claude Code and enterprise-focused workflows. This explains OpenAI's aggressive pivot to enterprise deployment. The AI market is becoming less about model quality and more about ecosystem control: infrastructure, deployment, integrations, consulting, and workflow ownership.

Read on Times Square Chronicles →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The G2 "Answer Economy" report is the biggest shift I've seen in buyer behavior data this year. If 51% of buyers are starting in ChatGPT and 69% are changing vendor selection based on what AI surfaces, your SEO strategy is now a lagging indicator. The companies showing up in AI answers are the companies getting on shortlists — before buyers ever visit your website.
